track_description: Exogenous efficiency improvement driven by IMO Carbon Intensity Indicator (CII) ratings requiring annual 2% fleet improvement. Background fleet renewal replaces old inefficient vessels with modern designs. Rate: -0.4%/yr compounding; multiplier 1.000 → 0.905 by 2050. Applies to conventional HFO and LNG transition states.
evidence_summary: IMO CII regulation (MEPC 80); IEA shipping decarbonisation 2023; DNV shipping forecast 2050; historical IMO fleet efficiency trend 2010-2023.
derivation_method: Energy coefficients from AES 2025 Table F1 international maritime bunkers (29.5 PJ, 2023-24) / estimated service 200,000 million_tkm = 147.5 GJ/million_tkm. Fuel split: 60% HFO (88.5), 40% MDO (59.0). NGGI check: 11.4 tCO2e/million_tkm × 200,000 / 1e6 = 2.3 MtCO2e.
double_counting_guardrail: Applies to conventional_hfo and lng_transition only. Zero-emission fuel state excluded (distinct technology pathway). Non-stacking with speed optimisation and wind-assist packages.
review_notes: CII rate may accelerate post-2030 as IMO 2050 targets bite; review against updated MEPC guidance.
